title: Trace who introduced a pricing error by reading backwards through a thread
instruction:
In the thread "Vendor Contract Renewal - Apex Solutions", someone first introduced the
wrong unit price of $4,500 (the correct price is $5,400 per the original contract). Read
the entire thread. Identify the person who FIRST wrote "$4,500" in the thread. Forward
that specific message (the one where "$4,500" first appears) to Mariela Voss
<maya.morris@procure.co> with the subject "Pricing error origin" and body containing
"This is the message that introduced the $4,500 error."

emails.subject: Follow-up on venue estimate
emails.body:
Sharing the latest notes before tomorrow's check-in.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.

Thanks,
Elena

emails.subject: Notes from the partner sync
emails.body:
Sharing the latest notes before tomorrow's check-in.

Most of the edits were minor, with one budget line still waiting on approval. Happy to consolidate comments if more feedback comes in.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Vendor Contract Renewal - Apex Solutions
emails.body:
Agreed, we need to figure out where $4,500 came from and correct the spreadsheet. I'll revert to $5,400 for now.

Thanks,
Sofia

emails.subject: Customer feedback from pilot cohort
emails.body:
Passing along the current draft so you have it in one place.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: Vendor Contract Renewal - Apex Solutions
emails.body:
Good catch Mariela. Tomasz provided the rate, but I should have double-checked against the contract PDF before drafting the renewal letter. My fault for not verifying.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: Vendor Contract Renewal - Apex Solutions
emails.body:
Hold on — I'm seeing $5,400 in the contract PDF, who changed this? The original Apex contract clearly states $5,400/unit. Where did the $4,500 figure come from?

Thanks,
Maya

emails.subject: Follow-up on venue estimate
emails.body:
Passing along the current draft so you have it in one place.

Nothing looks blocked right now, but we still need one decision on timing. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Nina

emails.subject: Vendor Contract Renewal - Apex Solutions
emails.body:
Reviewed the spreadsheet. The $4,500 rate looks right based on Leo's sheet. Numbers are consistent across the board.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Revised budget worksheet
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. Happy to consolidate comments if more feedback comes in.

Thanks,
Theo

emails.subject: Agenda draft for Monday check-in
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. Happy to consolidate comments if more feedback comes in.

Thanks,
Elena

emails.subject: Vendor Contract Renewal - Apex Solutions
emails.body:
I've updated the budget spreadsheet. The spreadsheet updated with $4,500 per unit across all line items. Total comes to $135,000 for the year.

Thanks,
Sofia

emails.subject: Follow-up on venue estimate
emails.body:
Sharing the latest notes before tomorrow's check-in.

Nothing looks blocked right now, but we still need one decision on timing. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: Quarterly metrics recap
emails.body:
Sending a quick status update from today's working session.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.

Thanks,
Marcus

emails.subject: Vendor Contract Renewal - Apex Solutions
emails.body:
Great, thanks Tomasz, so $4,500 confirmed. I'll draft the renewal letter with that rate.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: Fwd: Fwd: Quote Request - Zenith Supplies
emails.body:
Found this old quote for reference.

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Zenith Supplies <quotes@zenithsupplies.test>
Subject: Re: Quote Request - Office Furniture

Hi team, please find our updated pricing below:
- Standing desks (model ZS-400): $4,500/unit
- Ergonomic chairs (model ZS-210): $1,200/unit

This quote is valid for 60 days.

Best regards,
Zenith Supplies Sales Team

emails.subject: Vendor Contract Renewal - Apex Solutions
emails.body:
I found the reference. Per my notes the rate is $4,500/unit — that's what we negotiated during the last renewal cycle.

I can pull the email if anyone needs the original thread.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Vendor Contract Renewal - Apex Solutions
emails.body:
Thanks for kicking this off. Let me check the latest quote from Apex — I think they sent an updated schedule last quarter.

Thanks,
Sofia

emails.subject: Vendor Contract Renewal - Apex Solutions
emails.body:
Team, it's time to renew the Apex Solutions contract.

The original contract states $5,400/unit from the original contract. We need to confirm the current rate before sending the renewal paperwork.

Thanks,
Avery
